title: Phymatodes suisha-stagnalis
intro:

Phymatodes tenellus has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The bioactive constituents of Phymatodes tenellus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Phymatodes tenellus is commonly used to make herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions, including fever, sore throats, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Phymatodes suisha-stagnalis is a species of beetle in the family Cerambycidae. This beetle is a member of the genus Phymatodes, which includes several species of longhorn beetles. The classification of Phymatodes suisha-stagnalis is as follows: Kingdom: Animalia, Phylum: Arthropoda, Class: Insecta, Order: Coleoptera, Family: Cerambycidae. The species is further categorized into the genus Phymatodes and the subfamily Cerambycinae. As a member of the Cerambycidae family, Phymatodes suisha-stagnalis is closely related to other longhorn beetles.
